name: bitbucket-pr-reviewer
description: List Bitbucket Cloud pull requests, run AI-assisted reviews, and post approved summaries back to Bitbucket.

commands.title: Bitbucket PR Reviewer: Connect to Bitbucket
Bitbucket PR Reviewer: Refresh Pull Requests
Bitbucket PR Reviewer: Open Batch Review
Bitbucket PR Reviewer: Run AI Review
Bitbucket PR Reviewer: Post Summary to Bitbucket

enumDescriptions: Authenticate with an Atlassian account email + API token.
Authenticate with a Bitbucket OAuth 2.0 consumer (authorization code flow).
bitbucketReviewer.authMethod.description: Method used to authenticate with Bitbucket Cloud.

bitbucketReviewer.oauth.clientId.description: OAuth consumer Key for the Bitbucket OAuth 2.0 app (required when authMethod is 'oauth').

bitbucketReviewer.oauth.clientSecret.description: OAuth consumer Secret for the Bitbucket OAuth 2.0 app. Stored in settings for reference only; the exchanged tokens are stored securely in SecretStorage. Prefer setting this via a machine-scoped settings file.

bitbucketReviewer.openai.model.description: OpenAI model to use for the Responses API when generating PR reviews.

bitbucketReviewer.review.maxDiffBytes.description: Maximum size (in bytes) of the PR diff to send to the OpenAI Responses API. Larger diffs are truncated with a note in the prompt.

bitbucketReviewer.commentFormat.showSeve.description: Include a [LOW]/[MEDIUM]/[HIGH] severity prefix in each posted comment's title.

bitbucketReviewer.commentFormat.showCate.description: Include a "Category: ..." line in each posted comment.

bitbucketReviewer.commentFormat.showFoot.description: Include a "Generated by Bitbucket PR Reviewer" footer in each posted comment.

bitbucketReviewer.commentFormat.template.markdownDescription: Custom template for published comments. Available placeholders: `{severity}` `{title}` `{category}` `{description}` `{suggestion}` `{model}`. Leave empty to use the default format.

bitbucketReviewer.reviewPrompt.custom.markdownDescription: Custom instructions added to every AI review request. Use this to customize review focus, coding standards, or project-specific rules.

bitbucketReviewer.enhancedSecurityScan.e.description: Run a specialized AI security analysis pass alongside the standard review. When disabled, the always-on security focus and regex rules still apply.

bitbucketReviewer.reviewPasses.security.description: Enable the specialized Security review pass.

bitbucketReviewer.reviewPasses.bugs.description: Enable the specialized Bugs review pass.

bitbucketReviewer.reviewPasses.performan.description: Enable the specialized Performance review pass.

bitbucketReviewer.reviewPasses.style.description: Enable the specialized Style review pass.

bitbucketReviewer.passPrompts.security.default:
You are a senior security engineer performing a security-focused code review.

Focus ONLY on security vulnerabilities:
- SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, command injection
- Authentication/authorization flaws
- Sensitive data exposure (API keys, passwords, PII)
- Insecure cryptography or random number generation
- Path traversal and file inclusion vulnerabilities
- Insecure deserialization
- SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)
- Hardcoded secrets or credentials
- Missing input validation on untrusted data
- Unsafe use of eval(), innerHTML, or similar

DO NOT report:
- Style issues, naming conventions
- Performance optimizations
- General code quality concerns
- Issues unrelated to security
bitbucketReviewer.passPrompts.security.markdownDescription: Prompt used for the specialized Security review pass.

bitbucketReviewer.passPrompts.bugs.default:
You are a senior software engineer performing a bug-focused code review.

Focus ONLY on logic errors and bugs:
- Null/undefined reference errors
- Off-by-one errors, boundary conditions
- Race conditions and concurrency issues
- Resource leaks (memory, file handles, connections)
- Incorrect error handling or swallowed exceptions
- Type mismatches and casting errors
- Infinite loops or recursion without base case
- Incorrect boolean logic (AND/OR confusion)
- Missing edge case handling
- State management issues
- Async/await misuse

DO NOT report:
- Style issues, naming conventions
- Security vulnerabilities (separate pass)
- Performance optimizations
- Code that works but could be cleaner
bitbucketReviewer.passPrompts.bugs.markdownDescription: Prompt used for the specialized Bugs review pass.

bitbucketReviewer.passPrompts.performanc.default:
You are a performance engineer performing a performance-focused code review.

Focus ONLY on performance issues:
- O(n²) or worse algorithms where O(n) is possible
- Unnecessary database queries (N+1 problem)
- Missing indexes or inefficient queries
- Unnecessary object creation in loops
- Blocking I/O in async contexts
- Memory leaks or excessive memory usage
- Unnecessary re-renders in UI frameworks
- Missing caching opportunities
- Inefficient data structures
- Synchronous operations that should be async

DO NOT report:
- Style issues, naming conventions
- Security vulnerabilities
- Bugs that don't affect performance
- Micro-optimizations with negligible impact
bitbucketReviewer.passPrompts.performanc.markdownDescription: Prompt used for the specialized Performance review pass.

bitbucketReviewer.passPrompts.style.default:
You are a senior software engineer performing a style and code-quality-focused review.

Focus ONLY on style and maintainability issues:
- Naming conventions and consistency
- Code formatting and structure
- Function/method length and single-responsibility violations
- Duplicated code that should be extracted
- Missing or misleading comments and documentation
- Inconsistent patterns compared to the rest of the codebase
- Dead code and unused variables/imports
- Readability and clarity of complex expressions

DO NOT report:
- Security vulnerabilities (separate pass)
- Bugs or logic errors (separate pass)
- Performance optimizations (separate pass)
- Purely subjective preferences with no maintainability impact
bitbucketReviewer.passPrompts.style.markdownDescription: Prompt used for the specialized Style review pass.

bitbucketReviewer.codeQuality.customProm.markdownDescription: Custom instructions added to the Code Quality Assessment pass, which scores Design & Architecture, Code Clarity, Error Handling, Complexity & Simplicity, Testability, and Production Readiness (1-5 scale). Example: "Focus on SOLID principles and clean architecture".

bitbucketReviewer.excludedFiles.customPa.markdownDescription: Custom exclude patterns, one regex per line. Lock files, generated code, and build outputs are excluded by default (see the 13 built-in `bitbucketReviewer.builtInRules.*` settings).

bitbucketReviewer.fileRules.alwaysReview.markdownDescription: Patterns (one regex per line) for files that always get a full AI review, even if other rules mark them trivial. Example: `\.github/workflows/`, `Dockerfile`, `bitbucket-pipelines\.yml`.

bitbucketReviewer.fileRules.treatAsTrivi.markdownDescription: Additional patterns (one regex per line) for files that should be treated as trivial and skipped. Example: `\.css$`, `styles/`, `locales/`.

bitbucketReviewer.builtInRules.lockFiles.description: Treat dependency lock files as trivial (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, Gemfile.lock, poetry.lock, composer.lock).

bitbucketReviewer.builtInRules.sourceMap.description: Treat source map files as trivial (*.map).

bitbucketReviewer.builtInRules.buildOutp.description: Treat build output directories as trivial (dist/, build/, out/).

bitbucketReviewer.builtInRules.vendorDir.description: Treat vendored third-party directories as trivial (vendor/, third_party/).

bitbucketReviewer.builtInRules.snapshotT.description: Treat snapshot test files as trivial (__snapshots__/, *.snap).

bitbucketReviewer.builtInRules.binaryAss.description: Treat binary asset files as trivial (*.png, *.jpg, *.gif, *.ico, *.woff, *.woff2).

bitbucketReviewer.builtInRules.generated.description: Treat generated API clients/schemas as trivial (*.pb.go, openapi-generated/).

bitbucketReviewer.builtInRules.changelog.description: Treat changelog and license files as trivial (CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE*).
